title: Adopt the contribution as bee-herding: renamed, hardened, merge demoted to a gesture, dispatch interlocked
action: Read docs/history/herding-adopt/CONTEXT.md fully, including the advisor section — two of the orchestrator's own assumptions were disproven by measurement and the REVISED decisions are what govern. The contribution's 9 files are in `.bee/tmp/pr50/full.diff` (also `gh pr diff 50`). ALL SOURCE WORK HAPPENS HERE; the shipping switch (render + manifest) is h-2 (D3-REVISED). (A) RED-FIRST, capture and quote: place the files under their CONTRIBUTED name and run `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check` — quote the refusal, which is `release inventory names an unsafe managed skill: herdr-orchestrating` from `skills/bee-hive/scripts/plugin_distribution.mjs:135`, a NAME refusal, not a hash mismatch. Also capture the two reproduced shell defects on the contributed scripts BEFORE fixing: a trailing flag spins at 100% CPU forever (`--interval` with no value), and `--interval abc` produced 872 iterations in 4 seconds. (B) RENAME to `skills/bee-herding/` — the distribution hard-requires `^bee-[a-z0-9-]+$` and the render filters `/^bee-/`, so as contributed it ships to nobody. Rewrite every internal reference including the frontmatter `name`. Fix `bootstrap-cockpit.sh`'s hardcoded `.claude/skills/...` root to resolve from the script's own location (BASH_SOURCE/$SCRIPT_DIR, as `control-loop.sh` already does) — hardcoding breaks Codex and aborts every run here. (C) HARDEN (D3-REVISED): refuse a missing flag value instead of spinning (guard every `shift 2`, or `set -euo pipefail`); validate interval/timeout/max-iterations as positive integers; bound the loop with a consecutive-failure ceiling plus backoff and a default iteration cap, so a missing `claude` binary cannot produce an infinite 127 retry; add a turn ceiling to the control invocations (D12 — iterations were bounded, spend was not). (D) MERGE IS A GESTURE (D11, owner-confirmed): bootstrap starts ONLY the dispatch loop — remove the second `herdr pane run` that starts the merge loop. Merge runs single-shot via the `--once` flag the contribution already ships; document that as the harvest gesture. The stop gesture still gets fixed for dispatch: check the stop file before AND after each iteration, and state plainly in the docs that it does not stop already-running working agents. (E) INTERLOCK (D10): dispatch refuses to build a dispatchable set unless an owner-created enable marker exists. This is the highest-value change the advisor named — the previously-assumed 'safe failure' does not exist, two rows qualify today, and exploring manufactures more as normal operation. (F) POSTURE (D7-FINAL, owner-confirmed): working agents KEEP `bypassPermissions` — do not narrow them; record the accepted risk and its blast radius in the skill's own docs. Narrow the two CONTROL panes to an ENUMERATED COMMAND SURFACE, never 'read-only' — measured: dispatch creates a worktree and registers a grant, merge aborts against main, writes markers and runs cleanup. Read-only would stall both silently every 60s. (G) DOCS: correct the line claiming the loop 'will not pick up hard-gate work' — contradicted by the contribution's own text and by 8/8 measurement (D6). Strip or replace the 18 citations pointing at a history directory absent here, and scrub the contributor's absolute home paths (D9). (H) TESTS: a suite for the two reproduced shell defects, the interlock refusal, and that bootstrap starts one loop not two. Verify is the TARGETED suite per the verify ladder; h-2 runs the full chain.

truths: red evidence: the NAME refusal quoted verbatim, plus both reproduced shell defects captured before fixing
green: the new targeted suite passes, covering both shell defects, the interlock refusal, and one-loop bootstrap
bootstrap starts ONLY the dispatch loop, and the merge gesture is documented
dispatch refuses to select anything without the owner enable marker — asserted
working agents keep bypassPermissions with the accepted risk and blast radius written down; control panes carry an enumerated command surface, not read-only
no citation to a non-existent history directory and no contributor absolute paths remain
prohibitions: never relax the ^bee- constraint or the render filter to fit the skill — the constraint is the protection
do not narrow the working agents' permission mode (owner decision D7-FINAL)
do not run render_plugin_skill_trees or release_manifest --write here — that is the shipping switch, h-2 (D3-REVISED)
do not start any herdr pane, spawn any agent, or run the loop for real

trace.outcome: Adopted PR #50's herdr-based multi-session orchestration as bee-herding, design intact and implementation fixed. Renamed (mandatory: the distribution refuses any non-bee- name at install time for every user, and the render filters it out otherwise). Merge demoted from an unattended loop to a single-shot owner gesture (D11) — dropping the merge-authority hard-gate flag, the ~91-minute stop window, and the run-agent-code-via-verify exposure. Dispatch interlocked behind an owner enable marker (D10), because the assumed safe failure was measured not to exist: the classifier already parses this repo and two rows qualify today. Working agents keep bypassPermissions as a recorded accepted risk with blast radius stated (D7-final, owner decision); the two control panes narrowed to an enumerated command surface, never read-only, which was measured to stall them. The two reproduced shell defects (a trailing flag spinning at 100% CPU, a non-numeric interval hot-looping 871x in 4s) are fixed and pinned; the loop is now bounded by a failure ceiling and a turn cap. Targeted suite 10/10; the shipping switch and full chain are the next cell.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "Captured before any fix. NAME refusal (not a hash mismatch): plugin_distribution.mjs:135 managedSkillNames threw 'release inventory names an unsafe managed skill: herdr-orchestrating' with code DISTRIBUTION_REFUSED when the skill sat under its contributed name — the install-time refusal every user would hit, distinct from the manifest --check messenger. Two reproduced shell defects on the contributed control-loop.sh: a trailing flag (--interval with no value) spun until the 3s watchdog killed it (exit 124 = it spun), and --interval abc produced 871 iterations in 4 seconds. Both are the class the advisor measured.",
  "reported_cause_correction_carried_from_earlier": "This adoption exists because an earlier reading of mine said CI was red only over a stale manifest. That was wrong and is recorded as such: the manifest is the messenger; the real refusal is by NAME, and had it been forced green every plugin-first install would hard-refuse for every user. The render also filters /^bee-/, so as contributed the skill shipped to nobody.",
  "green_evidence": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_herding.mjs -> PASS 10 passed 0 failed, exit 0, auto-discovered by run_verify. Covers both trailing-flag refusals, non-numeric and zero interval refusals, the consecutive-failure ceiling (a missing-binary 127 retry now terminates instead of looping forever), the --once happy path, the interlock refusal (exit 3) and enable (exit 0), and that bootstrap starts exactly one dispatch loop and zero merge loops.",
  "rename_mandatory_not_cosmetic": "Renamed skills/herdr-orchestrating -> skills/bee-herding. Verified independently by the orchestrator: managedSkillNames now ACCEPTS the renamed set, and bootstrap-cockpit.sh resolves control-loop.sh from its own $SCRIPT_DIR rather than a hardcoded .claude/ root (which would break Codex and abort every run here).",
  "merge_demoted_to_gesture_D11": "Verified: the only live `herdr pane run` in bootstrap starts --role dispatch; the three `role merge` occurrences are a comment, a documented gesture command, and the idle-pane echo — none starts a loop. The merge pane is still created but left idle; merge runs single-shot via the shipped --once flag, documented as the owner harvest gesture. Confirmed by the suite (dispatch starts 1, merge loops 0).",
  "interlock_D10": "New dispatch-interlock.mjs. Run live against this repo with no marker: {enabled:false} exit 3, naming the exact `touch .bee/tmp/bee-herding.enable` the owner runs. This is the highest-value change the advisor named — the previously-assumed safe failure does not exist, two rows qualify today, and exploring manufactures more as normal operation. Dispatch builds nothing without the owner marker.",
  "posture_D7_final": "Owner decision honored exactly. Working agents KEEP bypassPermissions — not narrowed — with the accepted risk and its blast radius written into SKILL.md's Permission posture section (whole filesystem/network/credentials, up to four concurrent, worktree is a filesystem-and-git boundary not a sandbox, the lane filter chooses which item not what commands). The two CONTROL panes carry an ENUMERATED command surface via per-role --allowedTools, each entry mapped to a measured action (dispatch: worktree new + grant; merge: worktree merge --cleanup, git merge --abort against main, marker writes). Read-only was measured wrong — it would stall both roles silently every 60s. The coupling is recorded: the merge pane runs verify over the just-merged tree, executing code the working agents wrote.",
  "docs_D6_D9": "The false '.gitignore untracks bee-*' rationale removed (verified: skills/bee-herding is not ignored here). The 'will not pick up hard-gate work' over-promise corrected in both docs. Zero citations to the absent docs/history/agent-pane-orchestration directory and zero contributor absolute paths remain (verified by grep); the two recorded-proof transcripts carry explicit upstream-provenance notes rather than being silently rewritten as if verbatim here.",
  "verify_ladder": "This cell's verify is the TARGETED suite per the repo's verify ladder. The shipping switch (render + manifest + the hardcoded skill-count updates) and the full chain are the next cell (D3-REVISED: hardening precedes the shipping switch). The four currently-red suites are exactly that deferred drift — the manifest and the '15 bee-* skills' counts do not yet know bee-herding — not anything the new files broke; test_herding itself passes inside the full run.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Independently re-ran the suite, the interlock (exit 3), the trailing-flag fix (exit 1), and confirmed one live pane-run in bootstrap, zero herdr-orchestrating/vantt/history-dir leaks, and that the 4 red suites are all the deferred shipping switch. Worker's neutralized instruction-shaped output was treated as data. One residual noted for the next cell, non-blocking: the loop's internal descriptive name is still 'the agent-pane-orchestration loop' in a skill now called bee-herding — cosmetic, explained by the provenance notes.",
  "deviations_accepted": "Four, each reasoned. The interlock is a dedicated script rather than folded into the do-not-modify classify-lane. The loop gained --max-consecutive-failures/--turn-ceiling/a default iteration cap (D4/D12), which revises the contribution's 'unbounded/never exit' framing. Home paths scrubbed to a placeholder and broken history citations replaced with placeholders plus a provenance note. And the one honest limit: the control-pane allowlist surface is derived from the command inventory, not empirically exercised, because running claude was prohibited — the surface and a 'grow it with the role' caveat are documented, and this rests on the CLI's documented -p behavior rather than a local reproduction."
}

PASS  interlock: owner enable marker present → dispatch permitted (enabled:true, exit 0)

PASS  bootstrap: a trailing --workspace (no value) refuses, never spins
